figures.caption: Figure 5: Breslow validation: lifelines vs. KM-shift survival curve construction for Cox-PH. Both produce nearly identical calibration curves (∆p = 0.002). Random assignment serves as a negative control.

Figure 1: Calibration curves on TCGA-BRCA at median event time (∼42 months). Each subplot shows one model. Thick lines: mean across folds. Thin lines: individual folds. Dashed diagonal: perfect calibration. Cox-PH (positive control) tracks the diagonal; deep models deviate systematically.
Figure 4: Calibration curves before (blue) and after (orange) Platt scaling. Annotations show improvement in fold-level failure rates. MCAT shows the clearest visual improvement.

Figure 2: 1-calibration failure rates across 11 architectures and 5 TCGA cancer types. Each cell shows folds failed out of 5. Darker = more miscalibrated. GBMLGG column is almost entirely dark despite highest discrimination. Gating (sig) rows are consistently lighter than bilinear/concat rows within each family. UCEC is underpowered (∼15 events/fold).

Figure 3: Calibration curves across 5 TCGA cancer types for 4 representative architectures. GBMLGG shows the most dramatic deviation from the diagonal. UCEC shows all models clustered near the diagonal, consistent with underpowered testing.
